name: Rolled Stuffed Flank Steak
recipeInstructions:
1. Score the flank steak on both sides in a diagonal crosshatch pattern and rub with black pepper all over.
2. In a bowl, combine breadcrumbs, celery, onions, salt, thyme, half of the butter, white pepper, and enough water to form a thick paste. Mix well.
3. Spread the paste onto the flank steak, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges. Roll up lengthwise and secure with string.
4. Dredge the rolled steak in flour, patting off excess.
5. Heat 2 tablespoons butter in a Dutch oven over moderate flame. Add the rolled flank steak and brown on all sides.
6. Add water to cover about half of the steak, then reduce heat to very low. Cover and simmer for 2 hours.
7. Remove from the pot to a serving platter, remove string, and keep warm.
8. In a small bowl, mix ground ginger with equal parts cornstarch and water until smooth.
9. Return the steak to the Dutch oven, add the cornstarch mixture, and place over medium-low flame. Heat and stir until thickened.
10. Pour into a gravy boat and serve the flank steak hot, with gravy on the side.
